macro_segments.title: Market Valuation of Fields Without Fixed Value and the Role of Auction with Owner Participation

macro_segments.title: Gemara Analysis of Competing Bids: The Case Where Owner and Competitor Bid the Same Amount

Debate Over Precision: Whether the Mishnah Mentions Fractions and the Role of Rounding

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Yehuda's Teaching: When Higher Bid is Reinforced by Independent Assessment (hekdesh bi-shlosha)

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yehuda's Condition: The Higher Bid's Fifth is Based on Assessment Only if Hekdesh Made Independent Assessment
Distinction Between Assessment with Ten vs. Three and Its Effect on the Fifth
Baraita Evidence: Beit Shammai vs. Beit Hillel on Whether to Add Fifth Based on Higher Bid
Gemara's Resolution: No Assessment Was Made, Beit Shammai is Stringent in Requiring Fifth on Higher Bid

macro_segments.title: Final Case of the Mishnah: Owner's Option to Outbid and the Dinar Addition

micro_segments.title: The Mishnah's Apparent Grant of Last Chance to Owner When Outbid (30 and a dinar)
Rav Sheshet's Reinterpretation: The Higher Price Only if Owner Had Made Higher Initial Bid (me'ikara)
Dispute Over Meaning: Does Owner Get Post-Hoc Option to Increase Bid or Must Bid Be Initial?

macro_segments.title: Introduction to Cherem: The Laws of Dedicating Property as a Ban to the Beit Hamikdash

micro_segments.title: Distinction Between Cherem and Hekdesh: Cherem as Removing from Self vs. Hekdesh as Granting to Institution

Categories of Property Subject to Cherem: Flocks, Cattle, Servants, and Inherited Fields

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Eliezer's Principle: Cherem Cannot Be Made on All Property of One Type

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Eliezer's Position: Cherem Only Takes Effect on Part, Not All, of a Category
Rabbi Elazar ben Azaryah's Moral Reasoning: Even for God, One Should Not Sanctify Everything
Deriving the Rule from 'Mikol Asher Lo': From Everything But Not Everything

macro_segments.title: Gemara's Analysis: Why Cannot All Property of One Type Be Made Cherem?

micro_segments.title: Distinction Between Types of Property: Necessity-Based Limitation on Slaves vs. Fields
The Principle That You Cannot Make Cherem of Things You Don't Fully Own
Exclusion of Children, Hebrew Servants, and Purchased Fields: What Cannot Be Made Cherem

macro_segments.title: Special Status of Kohanim and Leviyim: Who Can and Cannot Make Cherem

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yehuda's Position: Kohanim Cannot Make Cherem Because They Are Entitled to Keep It
Rabbi Shimon's Counter: Kohanim Can Make Cherem of Chattel Since It Goes to Kohanim Anyway

Rabbi's Compromise: Yehuda Right for Land, Shimon Right for Chattel
